excluded_defs.name: streamWrite
excluded_defs.reason: read briefly to confirm it doesn't throw exceptions itself (just calls fwrite), but not needed for the fix

exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing try/finally only covered the error handler restoration. The outer lock acquisition had no corresponding finally for the unlock, meaning any exception from streamWrite() (or subclass override) would leave the lock held.
exploration_log.hindsight: The fix was straightforward once the retry path flow was understood. Reading close() first would have confirmed the is_resource() check was needed.

alternatives: Unlock without check (risks warning on closed resource)
Unlock before close() in the retry path (more invasive)
key_decisions.decision: Use is_resource($stream) check in finally block
key_decisions.reasoning: The retry path calls close() which fcloses $stream before returning, so without is_resource check the finally would call flock on a closed handle, triggering a PHP warning

search_sequence.reasoning: Task description pointed directly to StreamHandler.php and the lock/write section
search_sequence.result: Found flock(LOCK_EX) before write, but flock(LOCK_UN) only at the bottom after success — not in a finally block

search_sequence.action: Analyzed the retry path in write()
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to understand when the stream may already be closed before the finally block runs
search_sequence.result: In the retry path, close() is called which calls fclose() releasing the lock, then write() recurses. So the finally needs is_resource() check.

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: write
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: wrapped the write section in a try/finally to guarantee flock(LOCK_UN) is called

queries.justification: Rule compliance: uses only domain/business concepts — 'file locking mechanism', 'releases its lock', 'write operation fails'. Pre-implementation: a developer tasked with fixing a resource leak in a logging handler would search for this kind of pattern before knowing which file to edit.
queries.query_text: ensure a file locking mechanism releases its lock even when a write operation fails unexpectedly

queries.justification: Rule compliance: 'flock', 'LOCK_UN', and 'finally' all appear literally in the source. Pre-implementation: a developer who knows the fix involves a finally block around flock(LOCK_UN) would grep for this combination.
Rule compliance: all 5 are exact symbol/constant names from the changed code. Pre-implementation: a developer investigating the locking write path would search for these identifiers.

Rule compliance: names concrete symbol 'streamWrite' with relationship word 'callers'. Pre-implementation: a developer looking for where writes happen and locks are acquired would trace callers of the inner write method.

Rule compliance: includes explicit file path 'src/Monolog/Handler/StreamHandler.php'. Pre-implementation: the task description names StreamHandler.php directly, so a developer would navigate there first.

Rule compliance: mixes domain concept 'file lock not released when log write fails' with exact symbols 'flock' and 'streamWrite'. Pre-implementation: a developer who has read the bug report would naturally combine the symptom description with the relevant PHP function name.
queries.query_text: fix file lock not released when log write fails, involving flock and streamWrite

queries.justification: Rule compliance: includes symbols 'write' and 'useLocking' plus file path. Pre-implementation: once a developer identifies StreamHandler as the file, they'd refine by the method and property names.
Rule compliance: includes symbol names 'write', 'flock', 'LOCK_UN' and file path. Pre-implementation: this is the query a developer would type when starting the task based on the bug description.
queries.query_text: StreamHandler flock LOCK_UN not released on write failure, fix with finally block

solve_notes: Wrapped the write section in a try/finally block so that flock(LOCK_UN) is always called when useLocking is enabled. Added an is_resource($stream) check in the finally to avoid trying to unlock an already-closed stream (the retry path calls close() which fcloses the handle). This ensures the file lock is always released regardless of exception or error.
task_text:
Fix StreamHandler not releasing file lock on write failure

When `StreamHandler` acquires a lock via `flock()` before writing and
the `fwrite()` call fails (e.g., disk full), the lock is not released
because the exception bypasses the unlock path. Fix `StreamHandler.php`
to ensure `flock()` is released in a `finally` block regardless of
write outcome.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: close
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding that close() calls fclose() and sets stream to null is necessary to justify the is_resource() check in the finally block; without reading this an agent might not know the stream gets closed in the retry path

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set (write method) is what gets edited. The thrash_preventing set adds close() because an AI agent, when implementing the finally block, would need to verify whether the stream could be closed by the retry path before the finally runs. Without reading close(), the agent might either skip the is_resource() guard (producing PHP warnings) or add unnecessary unlock-before-close calls in the retry path.
